name: Azure Virtual Desktop Multi-Tenant Environment with OpenTofu
description: Build a production-grade Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) environment supporting shared multi-tenant hosting (RemoteApp only) and dedicated single-tenant deployments using OpenTofu with modular, DRY configurations, Hub-and-Spoke networking, AADDS, FSLogix, Azure Image Builder, and Checkov security scanning.

userStories.title: Fix known code defects and inconsistencies across modules
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want all known bugs and inconsistencies in the existing module code resolved so that `tofu validate` and `tofu plan` complete without errors across all layers.
acceptanceCriteria: `modules/networking/main.tf` line 127: fix `virtual_network_name = var.peering_config.remote_vnet_id` to reference the actual remote VNet name by adding `remote_vnet_name` field to `peering_config` variable object

`modules/fslogix/main.tf` lines 136-141: replace invalid `dynamic "virtual_network_subnet_ids"` block with `virtual_network_subnet_ids = var.subnet_id != "" ? [var.subnet_id] : []` as a direct attribute
`modules/aadds/main.tf` line 66: verify `azurerm_active_directory_domain_service_group_policy` exists in azurerm 4.x; if not, replace with `null_resource` + documented PowerShell approach
`modules/avd/main.tf` line 186: fix workspace association — workspace lookup key should be `each.value.name`, not `each.value.host_pool_name`
`bootstrap/main.tf` line 87: remove or fix incorrect self-assignment of storage account managed identity

`main.tf` lines 33-38: remove calls to `environments/shared` and `environments/dedicated` as child modules (they are root configs with own backends); add `WORKSPACES.md` explaining layered deployment order
All modules pass `tofu validate` with no errors after fixes
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Must be done first — FR-12 requires all known defects resolved before new feature work.

userStories.title: Extend bootstrap to provision full platform landing zone
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want the bootstrap layer to provision the state backend, Hub VNet with Azure Firewall, Private DNS Zones, management groups, and Log Analytics in a single `tofu apply`, so that the platform foundation is ready before any workload is deployed.
acceptanceCriteria: `bootstrap/main.tf` provisions: resource group, state storage account + container, management groups (`Landing Zones`, `Management`, `Connectivity`, `Shared`, `Dedicated`), Azure Policy (`CostCenter` tag audit)
`bootstrap/main.tf` provisions: Hub VNet (`10.0.0.0/16`), subnets `GatewaySubnet`, `AzureFirewallSubnet`, `snet-management`, `snet-frontend`, `snet-backend`
Azure Firewall (Standard SKU) with Firewall Policy (threat intel: Deny, IDS: Deny) deployed in hub
Private DNS Zones for `privatelink.file.core.windows.net` and `privatelink.blob.core.windows.net` created and linked to hub VNet
Log Analytics workspace `law-bootstrap-<env>` provisioned with correct retention
Microsoft Defender for Cloud enabled for `VirtualMachines`
OIDC service principal guidance documented in `BACKEND.md` with Service Principal app ID and required role assignments as outputs
All state backend outputs remain backward-compatible (no breaking changes)
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
Verify Checkov passes with no `CKV_AZURE_*` critical findings
Verify `tofu validate` succeeds within both `bootstrap/` and root module contexts
userStories.notes: Depends on US-011 to ensure existing bugs are fixed first.

userStories.title: Refactor Hub-and-Spoke networking into standalone platform layer
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the Hub-and-Spoke networking code moved into its own deployable root module under `networking/hub-and-spoke/` so that it can be versioned, planned, and applied independently from workloads.
acceptanceCriteria: `networking/hub-and-spoke/main.tf` manages all hub and static spoke VNets (hub, shared spoke, dedicated spoke template)
Hub-to-shared and hub-to-dedicated peerings are bidirectional in the same module
`azurerm_virtual_network_peering.reverse` bug fixed: references the actual VNet name of the remote network, not `remote_vnet_id`
`networking/hub-and-spoke/` has its own `backend.hcl` with state key `networking/hub-and-spoke`
Outputs export: `hub_vnet_id`, `shared_spoke_vnet_id`, `dedicated_spoke_vnet_id`, `hub_firewall_private_ip`, `subnet_ids` (map)
Private DNS Zone IDs exported for consumption by workload modules
`networking/hub-and-spoke/README.md` updated
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
Verify Checkov passes with no `CKV_AZURE_*` critical findings
Verify `tofu validate` succeeds within both `bootstrap/` and root module contexts
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 bootstrap being complete so hub resources exist.

userStories.title: Migrate session hosts from classic VMSS to Flexible VMSS
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want session hosts to use `azurerm_orchestrated_virtual_machine_scale_set` (Flexible Orchestration mode) so that AVD extension support, rolling upgrades, and zone-redundancy follow Microsoft's recommended deployment model.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove `azurerm_virtual_machine_scale_set` (classic) resource from `modules/avd/main.tf`
Add `azurerm_orchestrated_virtual_machine_scale_set` with: `platform_fault_domain_count = 1`, `single_placement_group = false`, `zones = ["1","2","3"]` (configurable)
OS disk configured: `storage_account_type = "Premium_LRS"`, `disk_size_gb` variable-driven
`os_profile` block uses `windows_configuration` sub-block (Flexible VMSS syntax)
`SystemAssigned` managed identity retained
AVD DSC extension (`Microsoft.PowerShell.DSC`) added as `azurerm_virtual_machine_scale_set_extension` for host pool registration, replacing custom PowerShell token injection
Domain join extension (`JsonADDomainExtension`) retained and chained after DSC extension via `provision_after_extensions`
`var.session_host_config` schema updated: remove `admin_password` field; add `enable_automatic_os_upgrade`, `zones`
`modules/dedicated/main.tf` `session_host_config` local updated to match new schema
`modules/avd/variables.tf` and `modules/avd/outputs.tf` updated accordingly
`tofu validate` passes in `environments/shared/` and `environments/dedicated/`
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on networking layer (US-002) so spoke subnets are available for VMSS placement.

userStories.title: Configure FSLogix profile containers via AADDS and Group Policy
userStories.description: As a security architect, I want FSLogix profile containers configured through AADDS Group Policy Objects so that session hosts automatically mount user profile VHDs without manual configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: `modules/aadds/main.tf`: verify or replace `azurerm_active_directory_domain_service_group_policy` — if it does not exist in azurerm 4.x, replace with `null_resource` + documented PowerShell/Azure CLI approach using `azurerm_virtual_machine_extension`
FSLogix registry keys documented/applied: `Enabled = 1`, `VHDLocations`, `DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply = 1`, `FlipFlopProfileDirectoryName = 1`
AADDS deployed in hub resource group with subnet `snet-aadds` (10.0.5.0/24) added to `networking/hub-and-spoke/main.tf`
AADDS subnet NSG allows: TCP 636 (LDAPS), TCP/UDP 389 (LDAP), TCP/UDP 88 (Kerberos), TCP/UDP 53 (DNS) from spoke subnets
AADDS DNS server IPs exported as outputs and injected into all spoke VNet DNS server settings
`modules/aadds/variables.tf` adds `aadds_subnet_id` variable; removes dependency on non-existent `azurerm_resource_group.this` inside module
`modules/fslogix/main.tf` `virtual_network_subnet_ids` dynamic block syntax corrected: use `virtual_network_subnet_ids = [var.subnet_id]` directly
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
Verify Checkov passes with no `CKV_AZURE_*` critical findings
Verify `tofu validate` succeeds within both `bootstrap/` and root module contexts
userStories.notes: Depends on networking layer being in place for AADDS subnet.

userStories.title: Implement per-customer Premium File Shares for FSLogix and App Attach
userStories.description: As a platform architect, I want each customer to have a dedicated Azure Premium File Share for FSLogix profiles and a separate shared Premium File Share for App Attach packages, so that customer profile data is isolated and application delivery is centralized.
acceptanceCriteria: `modules/storage/main.tf`: correct private endpoint `subresource_names` to `["file"]` only for FileStorage accounts (remove invalid `"blob"` entry)
Each shared environment customer gets: one `FileStorage` Premium storage account + one `profiles` file share (100 GiB minimum, Premium tier)
One additional `FileStorage` Premium storage account in the shared spoke for App Attach with file share named `appattach` (configurable quota)
`modules/dedicated/` verified consistent with above schema; `appattach` share support added via optional variable
All FSLogix storage accounts: `https_traffic_only_enabled = true`, `min_tls_version = "TLS1_2"`, `allow_nested_items_to_be_public = false`, network rules default `Deny` with Private Endpoint
Private endpoints deployed into `snet-shared-storage` (shared) or `snet-dedicated-storage` (dedicated) — subnet added to dedicated spoke if missing
Private DNS A-records registered in hub Private DNS Zone `privatelink.file.core.windows.net`
RBAC: `Storage File Data SMB Share Contributor` assigned to AADDS computer accounts / Entra groups per customer
Outputs expose storage account names, file share names, and private endpoint FQDNs
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
Verify Checkov passes with no `CKV_AZURE_*` critical findings
Verify `tofu validate` succeeds within both `bootstrap/` and root module contexts
userStories.notes: Depends on networking (US-002) for private endpoints and AADDS (US-006) for RBAC group assignments.

userStories.title: Configure shared RemoteApp-only host pool for multi-tenant LoB delivery
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want the shared AVD host pool configured as RemoteApp-only (no Published Desktop) with per-customer application groups, so that customers can only access the LoB application and not arbitrary desktop sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared host pool type: `Pooled`, `preferred_app_group_type = "RailApplications"`, load balancing: `BreadthFirst`
No `Desktop` application group created for the shared host pool
`azurerm_virtual_desktop_application_group` created with `type = "RemoteApp"` for the LoB application
`azurerm_virtual_desktop_application` resource created for the LoB application entry (name, path, command-line arguments as variables)
Per-customer Entra group can be assigned to the shared RemoteApp application group via `azurerm_role_assignment` (`Desktop Virtualization User`)
Scaling plan configured with `Weekdays` schedule: ramp up 07:00, peak 09:00-18:00, ramp down 18:00, off-peak 20:00
`environments/shared/customer.tf` updated: each customer entry maps to a role assignment on the shared RemoteApp application group
Workspace linked to RemoteApp application group
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on Flexible VMSS session hosts (US-003) and FSLogix storage (US-005) being in place.

userStories.title: Update Azure Image Builder pipeline for Flexible VMSS golden image
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the Image Builder pipeline updated so that the golden image produced is compatible with Flexible VMSS deployment and includes FSLogix configured for AADDS-joined profile paths.
acceptanceCriteria: `imaging/image-builder/main.tf`: image template `source` block changed from `"ImageVersion"` type to `"PlatformImage"` type with `publisher`, `offer`, `sku`, `version` variables
Shared Image Gallery image definition updated: `hyper_v_generation = "V2"`, `trusted_launch_enabled = true` for Flexible VMSS compatibility
Customization steps retained (Windows Update, Defender, Firewall, FSLogix, AVD optimizations); add step for FSLogix registry keys with placeholder profile path
Remove Chocolatey-based installation of `DirectoryServices-DomainController`; replace with `RSAT-AD-PowerShell` installation step only if needed
Add `sysprep` step at end: `type = "WindowsRestart"` + final `type = "PowerShell"` calling `sysprep.exe /generalize /oobe /shutdown /quiet`
`imaging/image-builder/variables.tf` adds: `source_image_publisher`, `source_image_offer`, `source_image_sku`, `source_image_version` (replaces `source_image_id`)
`imaging/image-builder/outputs.tf` exports `gallery_image_id` and `image_template_name`
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (Flexible VMSS) and US-006 (AADDS/FSLogix) being complete.

userStories.title: Add per-customer dedicated environment support with correct module composition
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the dedicated customer module to correctly compose networking, storage, AADDS integration, and AVD modules so that a new dedicated customer can be provisioned by adding a single call block in `environments/dedicated/`.
acceptanceCriteria: `modules/dedicated/main.tf` updated: `module.avd` call adds missing `domain_join_config` variable pass-through; adds `fslogix_config` pass-through
`modules/dedicated/main.tf`: `module.networking` uses `modules/networking`; `azurerm_virtual_network_peering.reverse` bug fixed so dedicated spoke peers correctly to hub
`modules/dedicated/variables.tf` adds: `hub_vnet_id`, `aadds_dns_servers` (list of IPs), `hub_firewall_private_ip`, `avd_image_id`
`modules/dedicated/main.tf` adds: `azurerm_route_table` + `azurerm_subnet_route_table_association` routing all spoke traffic through hub firewall (`0.0.0.0/0 -> var.hub_firewall_private_ip`)
`modules/dedicated/main.tf` adds: VNet DNS servers set to `var.aadds_dns_servers` on the spoke VNet
`environments/dedicated/customer-example.tf` updated to pass new required variables from networking layer outputs
`environments/dedicated/` has its own `backend.hcl` with state key `environments/dedicated`
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (shared AVD host pool), US-005 (FSLogix storage), and US-006 (AADDS).

userStories.title: Implement RBAC and IAM for multi-tenant isolation
userStories.description: As a security architect, I want fine-grained RBAC and IAM configured so that each customer in the shared environment can only access their own application group, file share, and data, with all assignments managed as code.
acceptanceCriteria: `modules/customer/main.tf` updated: `azurerm_role_assignment` for `Desktop Virtualization User` on customer's RemoteApp application group; `azurerm_role_assignment` for `Storage File Data SMB Share Contributor` on customer's FSLogix file share
`modules/customer/variables.tf` adds: `application_group_id`, `fslogix_storage_account_id`, `customer_entra_group_object_id`
Managed identity (System Assigned) on session hosts granted `Storage File Data SMB Share Contributor` on shared FSLogix storage accounts
Service principal for OpenTofu automation documented in `BACKEND.md` with minimum required roles
All `azurerm_role_assignment` resources include `skip_service_principal_aad_check = false` where applicable
Audit diagnostic settings: `azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting` added to Log Analytics for AVD host pools and application groups
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (application groups) and US-005 (storage accounts) being in place.

userStories.title: Implement Checkov integration (pre-commit and CI pipeline)
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want Checkov scanning integrated into both local pre-commit hooks and a CI pipeline so that all OpenTofu configurations are validated for security misconfigurations before merge and before deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: `.pre-commit-config.yaml` created at repo root with: `checkov` hook targeting `--framework terraform --compact --quiet`; `tofu fmt` hook; `tflint` hook
`checkov-config.yaml` created with: `framework: terraform`, `output: cli,json`, `output-file-path: reports/checkov`, `skip-check: []`

GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/checkov.yml` created: triggers on `push` and `pull_request` to `main`; runs `tofu fmt -check`, `tofu validate`, `tflint --recursive`, `checkov -d . --framework terraform --compact`; uploads Checkov JSON report as artifact
All existing `#checkov:skip=CKV_AZURE_33` inline suppressions in `imaging/image-builder/main.tf` documented with justification comments
`reports/` directory added to `.gitignore`
`CHECKOV.md` created documenting: local run instructions, CI integration, suppression policy, list of suppressed checks with justifications
All modules pass Checkov with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Can be done after US-011 (code defects fixed) so scans don't fail on known bugs.

userStories.title: Integrate Azure Verified Modules for networking and storage
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want to evaluate and integrate Azure Verified Modules for VNet, storage, and AVD components so that we leverage Microsoft-supported, well-tested patterns where they reduce custom code.
acceptanceCriteria: Research AVM registry: `azure/avm-res-network-virtualnetwork`, `azure/avm-res-storage-storageaccount`, `azure/avm-res-desktopvirtualization-hostpool`
For each evaluated AVM: document in `AVM.md` whether adopted or rejected, and why
If AVM for VNet adopted: replace `modules/networking/main.tf` VNet + subnet resources with AVM call; wrap in thin module if customization needed
If AVM for storage adopted: replace `modules/storage/main.tf` storage account resources with AVM call
All AVM sources pinned to a specific version tag (no `latest`)
`.terraform.lock.hcl` updated after AVM integration
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (networking) and US-005 (storage) being complete before evaluating replacements.

userStories.title: Improve documentation and add operational runbooks
userStories.description: As an operations team, I want comprehensive, updated documentation reflecting all architectural decisions and correct deployment order so that the environment can be deployed, operated, and extended without expert assistance.
acceptanceCriteria: `README.md` updated: architecture overview, hub-and-spoke topology, module map, deployment order (bootstrap → networking → imaging → shared/dedicated)
`WORKSPACES.md` updated: explains each OpenTofu workspace/root module, its state key, prerequisites, and deployment command
`BACKEND.md` updated: OIDC setup instructions for GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps; required service principal roles
`docs/runbook-add-customer.md` created: step-by-step for adding a new shared customer and a new dedicated customer
`docs/runbook-image-update.md` created: how to trigger a new golden image build and roll out to session hosts
`docs/architecture-decisions.md` created: captures decisions (shared RemoteApp only, Flexible VMSS, AADDS in hub, Checkov dual integration)
All `.tf` files have module-level description comments at top
`tofu fmt -check` passes
`tofu validate` passes
`tofu plan` completes without errors
`checkov -d . --framework opentofu --compact` passes with 0 unsuppressed critical/high findings or documented suppressions
`tflint --recursive` passes
userStories.notes: Documentation story — can be done last once all implementation stories are complete.
